name: Szechuan Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 chicken breast halves, boned and skinned (or other meat--see above)
3 tablespoons soy sauce (we use low-salt soy sauce)
1 teaspoon vinegar (we usually use rice or rice wine vinegar, but cider vinegar also works)
1 clove minced garlic (optional, but we always use it)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix 2 teaspoons sugar, 1 teaspoon vinegar, 1 tablespoon dry sherry, and 2 tablespoons soy sauce.
2. In another bowl, mix 1 tablespoon soy sauce and 1 tablespoon cornstarch.
3. Cut the chicken into 1/2 to 1-inch pieces and combine with the soy sauce/cornstarch mixture.
4. Heat a wok and add about 2 tablespoons oil.
5. Add red pepper flakes and cook for about 10 seconds, then add the meat and stir-fry for about 1 minute. Remove the meat from the wok.
6. Stir-fry ginger, green onions, and garlic in the remaining oil for about 1 minute.
7. Return the meat to the wok and add the sugar/vinegar/sherry/soy sauce mixture. Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
8. Serve over hot rice.
